method: Stratified site-register permutation test on structural word-length (L2). For each testable document class (support classes with >=2 sites having >=30 words), compute tie-corrected Kruskal-Wallis H on word-length grouped by SITE; combined statistic = sum of per-class H. STRATIFIED null permutes SITE labels WITHIN each class (preserves class word-length multiset and each site's n-in-class), >=2000 draws. Contrast: raw (uncontrolled) site KW over all words to quantify class-confounding. PC synthetic (planted residual site-register effect + class-only false-positive).
result: STRATIFIED combined site effect on word-length is highly significant (combined perm p=0.0000) and ALL THREE testable classes individually show a significant within-class site effect (Roundel p=0.0000, Stone vessel p=0.0000, Tablet p=0.0000). PC passed. Word-length carries a GENERAL residual site register BEYOND document class. The raw (uncontrolled) site H=223.0 is much larger than the stratified combined H=89.6, indicating substantial class-confounding (~60% of raw site effect is class), but a large residual (~40%) survives as genuine within-class site variation.

contrast_raw_vs_stratified.interpretation: Raw site effect is ~2.5x the stratified combined H; majority class-confounded but large residual site register survives within class.

key_findings: CONTROLLING for document class, word-length still carries a significant residual SITE effect: the stratified combined KW H=89.6 (perm p=0.0000 over 2000 draws), so word-length is NOT fully determined by document function.

The residual site register is GENERAL, not class-specific: all 3 testable classes individually show a significant within-class site effect (Tablet p=0.0000, Stone vessel p=0.0000, Roundel p=0.0000), satisfying the >=2-class criterion for WORDLEN_SITE_REGISTER.

Substantial class-confounding exists: the raw (uncontrolled) site H=223.0 is ~2.5x the stratified combined H=89.6, so ~60% of the apparent raw site effect is actually document-class composition (e.g., Iouktas/Palaikastro are Stone-vessel-heavy with long words; Haghia Triada/Khania are Tablet-heavy with short words). The stratified null removes this and a ~40% residual remains.

Within Tablet (the largest class), Khania has markedly shorter words (mean 1.50) than Phaistos (2.05) or Tylissos (1.93); within Stone vessel, Zakros is much shorter (2.17) than Iouktas (3.76) or Palaikastro (3.21); within Roundel, Haghia Triada (2.47) is longer than Khania (1.62). Site-specific scribal register is consistent across classes.

Positive control PASSED: the machinery detects a planted +1 residual site-register shift (combined p=0.0) and does NOT fire on class-only data (0/20 false positives), so the verdict is machinery-informative.
successor_hypotheses: E066: Is the residual within-class site register driven by a few high-frequency sign-sequences (formulaic word-shapes) that differ in rate across sites, or by a broad shift of the whole word-length distribution? Decompose via per-site word-length histograms within class.

E067: Does the site register survive when controlling for inscription sub-context (e.g., administrative vs votive within Tablet) in addition to support class, or is part of the 'site' effect actually sub-context confounding?

E068: Is the site word-length register correlated with the site vocabulary/typology register (site-local side of the dichotomy), i.e., do sites with longer words also have distinct vocabularies — a unified site-local scribal profile?

E069: Per-site rank-shift analysis: rank sites by mean word-length within each class and test whether the site ranking is concordant across classes (e.g., is Khania always shortest?), which would indicate a stable cross-class site register rather than class-specific noise.
E070: Power/robustness: re-run with a higher per-site word threshold (>=50, >=100) and leave-one-site-out to confirm the residual site effect is not dominated by a single outlier site per class.
E071: Decompose the raw-vs-stratified gap: quantify how much of the 223->89.6 reduction is attributable to each (class, site) cell to map exactly where class-confounding is largest.
non_circular: word-length = len(signs) (structural count only; no sign values, no readings, no meaning). document class = corpus 'support' field (physical, given). site = corpus 'site' field (findspot, given). The stratified null permutes site labels WITHIN each class, preserving the class word-length multiset and each site's n-in-class, so any detected effect is site variation BEYOND class. No derived/semantic feature is used. L2 only.
